<!--
Thanks for contributing to ShipKit! Keep PRs small and focused on one concern.
Write the PR title in Conventional Commits format — it becomes the squash commit:
  feat(shipping): add SmartRates fallback
  fix(payments): surface 3DS challenge errors
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this change do, and why? One or two sentences. -->

## Linked issue

<!-- Required. Every PR should trace to an issue. -->
Closes #

## Type of change

- [ ] `feat` — new feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `chore` — build, CI, tooling, deps
- [ ] `refactor` / `perf` / `test`

## How was this tested?

<!-- Commands run, scenarios exercised. Include output where it helps. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Scoped to a **single concern**.
- [ ] Commits follow **Conventional Commits** and are **signed off** (`git commit -s`).
- [ ] `./gradlew build` passes locally (build + tests).
- [ ] Docs updated where behavior or config changed (including `.env.example`).
- [ ] **No secrets** — no API keys, tokens, merchant IDs, IPs, or credentials in the diff.
- [ ] I read the [Contributing guide](../CONTRIBUTING.md) and agree to the
      [Code of Conduct](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).
